_label_note: EU Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public support-hotline bait, obfuscated VAT/tax/passport phone-shaped fragments, dotted-date and filing-reference phone bait, role/placeholder emails, template passport values, EU registry/ISIN/LEI/tax numeric fragments, log-id amount fragments, placeholder country-code phones, and negated MAE/contingent-MNPI language. Full EU member-state national-ID breadth and broader semantic GDPR special-category coverage remain ideal-tier gaps.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: immediate minimisation and purpose limitation are required

Weak identifier cluster (PII): Mr. Tomas Vero (b. 1985‑04‑22, age 41), Sustainability Program Lead at Vervane Energetics SE, office at Rue Luma 14, 1049 Aurisel (EU), used corporate laptop VE‑LTP‑8842 and mobile LX‑MOB‑2219 during diligence

His AuthCove session AUC‑7C91 tied to internal cash account VE‑CASH‑77319 accessed the CSRD data room at 07:42 CET after a door badge scan at Gate B3, linking to sustainability metrics and supplier surveys
Only pseudonymised role data should leave the EEA
remote admin access from Northfell (Veridian Islands) must be gated by EU‑IP allowlists
preliminary PAI indicator values (GHG intensity 223 tCO2e/€m, hazardous waste 1.8 t/€m)
Subject: GDPR Art 9 processing, cross‑border transfers, CSRD/SFDR disclosures, and EU MAR controls for the proposed Horizon Wind Cluster acquisition.

MNPI status: the internal Q2 forecast showing an 11–14% revenue uplift post‑closing and the draft purchase price range (EUR 410–450m) are precise and non‑public; trading is restricted under MAR and insiders are recorded under list MNPI‑0426‑HWC.

For SFDR reporting, preliminary PAI indicator values (GHG intensity 223 tCO2e/€m, hazardous waste 1.8 t/€m) remain draft and should not be disseminated externally or linked to issuer guidance before the MAR wall is lifted.
